name: Toolbar
description: A container for grouping action buttons, links, and toggle controls with shared keyboard navigation. Implements the WAI-ARIA toolbar pattern with roving tabindex. Built on Base UI Toolbar with StyleX styling.

Root.description: The toolbar container. Renders role="toolbar" with aria-orientation. Manages a roving tabindex across child items so only one item is in the tab order at a time; arrow keys move focus between items.

orientation.description: The orientation of the toolbar. Drives aria-orientation and the arrow-key axis (Left/Right for horizontal, Up/Down for vertical). RTL automatically inverts the horizontal axis.

disabled.description: Whether the entire toolbar is disabled.

loopFocus.description: Whether arrow-key navigation wraps from the last item back to the first.

dataAttributes.data-orientation: The orientation of the toolbar ('horizontal' or 'vertical').
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the toolbar is disabled.

Button.description: A focusable action button registered with the toolbar's roving tabindex.

focusableWhenDisabled.description: Whether a disabled button remains keyboard-focusable (recommended for toolbars per WAI-ARIA).

dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the button is disabled.

Link.description: A focusable anchor registered with the toolbar's roving tabindex.

dataAttributes.data-orientation: The orientation of the parent toolbar.

Group.description: Visually groups related toolbar items. Renders role="group".

disabled.description: Disables every item inside the group.

dataAttributes.data-orientation: The orientation of the parent toolbar.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the group is disabled.

Separator.description: An accessible separator between toolbar items. Renders role="separator" with the correct orientation.

dataAttributes.data-orientation: The orientation of the separator (perpendicular to the toolbar).

ToggleGroup.description: Groups a set of Toolbar.ToggleItem buttons sharing pressed state. Single- or multi-select.

value.description: Controlled list of pressed item values.

onValueChange.description: Fired when the pressed set changes.

multiple.description: When true, multiple items can be pressed simultaneously.

disabled.description: Disables every item in the group.

dataAttributes.data-orientation: The orientation of the toggle group.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the group is disabled.

ToggleItem.description: A two-state toggle button inside a Toolbar.ToggleGroup. Rendered through Toolbar.Button so it participates in the toolbar roving tabindex.

value.description: Unique identifier used by the parent ToggleGroup to track pressed state.

dataAttributes.data-pressed: Present when the toggle is pressed.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the toggle is disabled.

intents.reasoning: Toolbar groups action buttons with WAI-ARIA roving tabindex, so a single Tab stop reaches the bar and arrow keys move between actions.
ToggleGroup with multiple=true supports independent pressed states for bold/italic/underline-style controls.
Single-select ToggleGroup (multiple=false) is ideal for mutually exclusive choices like text alignment.
orientation="vertical" lays the toolbar out as a column and remaps arrow keys to Up/Down for focus traversal.

avoidWhen.scenario: A bar of dropdown menus (File, Edit, View)
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Menubar — its keyboard model and menu popup integration are tuned for menu triggers, not action buttons.
Use NavigationMenu — toolbars are for in-app actions, not page-level navigation links.
If you need just one on/off button, the ToggleGroup wrapper is unnecessary overhead. Use Button with an aria-pressed pattern.

examples.name: with-toggles
examples.code:
<Toolbar.Root>
  <Toolbar.ToggleGroup multiple defaultValue={["bold"]}>
    <Toolbar.ToggleItem value="bold">B</Toolbar.ToggleItem>
    <Toolbar.ToggleItem value="italic">I</Toolbar.ToggleItem>
    <Toolbar.ToggleItem value="underline">U</Toolbar.ToggleItem>
  </Toolbar.ToggleGroup>
  <Toolbar.Separator />
  <Toolbar.Link href="#docs">Docs</Toolbar.Link>
</Toolbar.Root>
